storyTitle: Rework quality scaling to preserve full-screen while reducing GPU cost

tests.name: QualityProfile includes shaderComplexity for all tiers
tests.description: Low tier has shaderComplexity='low', noiseOctaves=1, enablePointerRepulsion=false, enableSlowModulation=false
tests.setup: Use makeSignals with low-end phone values (DPR=1, cores=2, memory=1, 320x568, touch=true)

tests.name: Medium tier has shaderComplexity='medium' with noiseOctaves=2
tests.description: Medium tier profile has expected shader complexity values with pointer repulsion and slow modulation enabled
tests.setup: Use makeSignals with mid-range phone values (DPR=2, cores=4, memory=4, 390x844, touch=true)

tests.name: High tier has shaderComplexity='high' with noiseOctaves=3 and all features enabled
tests.description: High tier profile enables all shader features at maximum quality
tests.setup: Use makeSignals with high-end desktop values (DPR=2, cores=16, memory=8, 2560x1440, touch=false)

tests.name: Canvas CSS dimensions always fill viewport regardless of quality tier
tests.description: For each tier, after initScene with that tier's resolutionScale, renderer.domElement.style.width === '100%' and style.height === '100%'
tests.setup: Call initScene with a container div and each tier's resolutionScale. Check domElement.style.width and height after init.

tests.name: Low-tier renderer pixel ratio is smaller than high-tier while CSS size is identical
tests.description: renderer pixel ratio with low resolutionScale < renderer pixel ratio with high resolutionScale, while CSS size remains 100%
assertions: low-tier effective pixel ratio < high-tier effective pixel ratio
both tiers have style.width === '100%' and style.height === '100%'
tests.setup: Create two renderers with resolutionScale 0.5 and 1.0. Compare setPixelRatio calls. Both should have CSS 100%.

tests.name: Shader uniforms reflect quality config for point cloud
tests.description: createPointCloud with noiseOctaves=1 produces material with uNoiseOctaves=1, and other shader complexity uniforms match config
tests.setup: Create point cloud with explicit noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation config. Init with scene and check uniforms on the added mesh's material.

tests.name: Shader uniforms reflect quality config for ribbon field
tests.description: createRibbonField with enablePointerRepulsion=false produces material with uEnablePointerRepulsion=0.0
tests.setup: Create ribbon field with shader complexity config. Init and inspect mesh material uniforms.

tests.name: Low-tier point cloud has <= maxPoints particles
tests.description: Re-verifies point count cap with new config shape including shader complexity fields
tests.setup: Compute low-tier quality, create point cloud with full quality config including shader complexity fields, verify count.

tests.name: Visual identity preserved: all tiers produce non-zero point counts
tests.description: For low/medium/high, pointCount > 0 and geometry has positions — ensures the scene is recognisable across tiers
assertions: positions array is non-null and non-empty for each tier
tests.setup: For each tier's signal set, compute quality, create point cloud, init, and check pointCount and positions.

tests.name: No localStorage or cookie access during quality-scaled rendering with shader complexity config
tests.description: Privacy check across all tiers with new shader complexity config fields
assertions: localStorage.getItem not called during quality computation, init, or draw
document.cookie not accessed during quality computation, init, or draw
tests.setup: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem and document.cookie. Compute quality, create point cloud and ribbon with full config, init and draw, then verify no storage access.

changes.description: Add assertions for new QualityProfile fields on high tier
Add assertions for new QualityProfile fields on medium tier
Add assertions for new QualityProfile fields on low tier
Add range checks for new fields
Pass new config fields to createParticleField
changes.note: Add noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation to config object if particleField accepts them

changes.description: Pass new config fields to createRibbonField
changes.note: Add noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation to config object

changes.description: Pass new config fields to both geometry systems in privacy test
changes.note: Add noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation to both createParticleField and createRibbonField configs

imports: import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';

sharedSetup.defaultParams:
const defaultParams: VisualParams = {
  paletteHue: 180,
  paletteSaturation: 0.5,
  cadence: 0.7,
  density: 0.6,
  motionAmplitude: 1.0,
  pointerDisturbance: 0,
  bassEnergy: 0,
  trebleEnergy: 0,
  curveSoftness: 0.5,
  structureComplexity: 0.5,
  noiseFrequency: 1.0,
  radialScale: 1.0,
  twistStrength: 1.0,
  fieldSpread: 1.0,
};
